# umami

> Umami Analytics Client for Python

## Docs

### API Reference

#### Configuration
> One-time setup. `set_url_base()` is required before any operation; Cloud users call `set_cloud_api_key()` instead.

- [set_url_base](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/set_url_base.html): Each Umami instance lives somewhere. This is where yours lives
- [set_website_id](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/set_website_id.html): Your Umami instance might have many websites registered for various domains you use
- [set_hostname](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/set_hostname.html): The default hostname for sending events (can be overriden in the new_event() function)
- [set_cloud_api_key](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/set_cloud_api_key.html): Authenticate against Umami Cloud with an API key instead of login()
- [clear_cloud_api_key](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/clear_cloud_api_key.html): Exit Cloud mode and return to token/self-hosted behavior
- [enable](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/enable.html): Enable event and page view tracking
- [disable](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/disable.html): Disable event and page view tracking

#### Authentication
> Required for the query endpoints and `verify_token`. Not needed to send events.

- [login](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/login.html): Logs into Umami and retrieves a temporary auth token. If the token is expired,
- [login_async](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/login_async.html): Logs into Umami and retrieves a temporary auth token. If the token is expired,
- [is_logged_in](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/is_logged_in.html): Whether a credential is currently set locally
- [verify_token](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/verify_token.html): Verifies that the token set when you called login() is still valid. Umami says this token will expire,
- [verify_token_async](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/verify_token_async.html): Verifies that the token set when you called login() is still valid. Umami says this token will expire,

#### Sending events
> Send custom events, revenue, and page views. No login required — only a URL base and a website id/hostname.

- [new_event](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/new_event.html): Creates a new custom event in Umami for the given website_id and hostname (both use the default
- [new_event_async](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/new_event_async.html): Creates a new custom event in Umami for the given website_id and hostname (both use the default
- [new_revenue_event](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/new_revenue_event.html): Creates a new revenue event in Umami. This is a convenience wrapper around new_event()
- [new_revenue_event_async](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/new_revenue_event_async.html): Creates a new revenue event in Umami. This is a convenience wrapper around new_event_async()
- [new_page_view](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/new_page_view.html): Creates a new page view event in Umami for the given website_id and hostname (both use the default
- [new_page_view_async](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/new_page_view_async.html): Creates a new page view event in Umami for the given website_id and hostname (both use the default

#### Querying stats
> Read analytics back out. Requires login (or a Cloud API key).

- [websites](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/websites.html): All the websites that are registered in your Umami instance
- [websites_async](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/websites_async.html): All the websites that are registered in your Umami instance
- [website_stats](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/website_stats.html): Retrieves the statistics for a specific website
- [website_stats_async](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/website_stats_async.html): Retrieves the statistics for a specific website
- [active_users](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/active_users.html): Retrieves the active users for a specific website
- [active_users_async](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/active_users_async.html): Retrieves the active users for a specific website

#### Health
> Check Umami server connectivity.

- [heartbeat](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/heartbeat.html): Verifies that the server is reachable via the internet and is healthy
- [heartbeat_async](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/heartbeat_async.html): Verifies that the server is reachable via the internet and is healthy

#### Response models
> Pydantic models returned by the query functions.

- [models.WebsitesResponse](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/models.WebsitesResponse.html)
- [models.Website](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/models.Website.html)
- [models.WebsiteStats](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/models.WebsiteStats.html)
- [models.WebsiteStatsCmp](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/models.WebsiteStatsCmp.html)
- [models.LoginResponse](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/models.LoginResponse.html)
- [models.User](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/models.User.html)
- [models.WebsiteUser](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/models.WebsiteUser.html)

#### Errors
> Exception hierarchy. `ValidationError` is the base; `OperationNotAllowedError` is raised when required state (url_base, login) is missing.

- [errors.ValidationError](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/errors.ValidationError.html)
- [errors.OperationNotAllowedError](https://mkennedy.codes/docs/umami-python/reference/errors.OperationNotAllowedError.html)
